findings.title: FolderMonitor, DocumentProcessor, chunkText have no live consumer
findings.code_snippet:
export { FolderMonitor } from './capture/folder-monitor'
export { DocumentProcessor } from './processing/document-processor'
export { chunkText } from './processing/text-chunker'
findings.description: All three new modules are exported from the package barrel but no application or service imports them. The only references are in test files within the same package. No app in apps/ (stage-tamagotchi, stage-web, server) imports FolderMonitor, DocumentProcessor, or chunkText. This is a horizontal-only addition with no live entry point.
findings.suggestion: Wire FolderMonitor and DocumentProcessor into at least one live entry point (e.g., stage-tamagotchi orchestrator service) to form a vertical slice. At minimum, add a TODO-free integration in the Electron main process that watches a user-configured folder and indexes documents on change.

findings.title: No structured logging in FolderMonitor or DocumentProcessor
findings.description: Neither FolderMonitor nor DocumentProcessor use the project's @guiiai/logg structured logging. File watcher lifecycle events (start, stop, error, subscription count) and document extraction operations (file processed, extraction time, failures) are completely invisible in production. The onError callback is the only error path, but watcher start/stop and successful operations produce zero observability.
findings.suggestion: Add `import { useLogg } from '@guiiai/logg'` and log key lifecycle events: watcher start/stop with directory count, file change events at debug level, extraction start/completion with duration_ms, and all error paths with context.

findings.title: DocumentProcessor.extractText has no path validation
findings.description: extractText accepts an arbitrary file path with no validation against path traversal or symlink attacks. If this module is ever exposed via an API or user input (e.g., user picks a folder to watch, and FolderMonitor feeds paths to DocumentProcessor), an attacker could craft symlinks pointing outside the watched directory to read sensitive files like /etc/passwd or credentials. The file path is passed directly to readFile without any containment check.
findings.suggestion: Add a `basePaths` or `allowedRoots` parameter to DocumentProcessor and validate that resolved paths (via path.resolve + fs.realpath) stay within the allowed directory tree before reading. At minimum, resolve symlinks and verify containment.

findings.title: No file size limit before reading entire file into memory
findings.description: extractPdf, extractDocx, and extractPlainText all call readFile() on user-provided file paths with no size check. A multi-gigabyte PDF or a maliciously large file would be read entirely into memory, causing OOM crashes in the Electron process. This applies to all four extraction methods (lines 50, 84, 99, 129).
findings.suggestion: Add a configurable maxFileSizeBytes option (default e.g. 100MB) and stat the file before reading. Reject files exceeding the limit with a descriptive error. For very large text files, consider streaming.

findings.title: Unsafe `as` casts in PDF text extraction
findings.description: Triple `as` cast chain to extract the str property from pdfjs-dist TextItem. The pdfjs-dist types export TextItem with a str property directly. Using Record<string, unknown> obscures the actual type and bypasses type safety. If the pdfjs-dist API changes, the runtime 'in' check would still pass but the cast could be wrong.
findings.suggestion: Import TextItem from pdfjs-dist types and use a proper type guard: `import type { TextItem } from 'pdfjs-dist/types/src/display/api'`. Then check `if ('str' in item) { return (item as TextItem).str }` or use the hasOwnProperty pattern with a single targeted cast.

findings.title: Unsafe `as` cast on Excel row.values
findings.code_snippet: const cellValues = row.values as (string | number | boolean | null | undefined)[]
findings.description: ExcelJS row.values can contain Date objects, RichText objects, Error objects, and formula results -- not just string|number|boolean|null|undefined. Casting to a narrow union type and then calling String(v) on a Date will produce locale-dependent output (e.g., 'Mon Feb 20 2026 ...' instead of an ISO date). Formula error objects would produce '[object Object]'.
findings.suggestion: Use a proper mapping function that handles all ExcelJS CellValue types: Date (toISOString), RichText (extract text parts), CellErrorValue, CellHyperlinkValue, etc. Or use `cell.text` which ExcelJS provides as a pre-formatted string.

findings.title: No end-to-end path connecting FolderMonitor to DocumentProcessor
findings.description: FolderMonitor and DocumentProcessor are designed to work together (monitor detects file changes, processor extracts text), but there is no code that connects them. The integration test (document-pipeline.integration.test.ts) tests DocumentProcessor -> chunkText -> VectorStore but does NOT include FolderMonitor in the pipeline. There is no walking skeleton showing the full file-watch -> extract -> chunk -> store flow.
findings.suggestion: Either create a DocumentPipeline orchestrator that wires FolderMonitor.onChange to DocumentProcessor.extractText -> chunkText -> VectorStore.insert, or extend the integration test to include FolderMonitor triggering the pipeline on file creation.

findings.title: Error messages expose full file system paths
findings.description: Error messages include the full absolute file path. While this is useful for debugging, if these errors propagate to a UI or API response, they could leak internal directory structure information. Currently low risk since context-engine is a library, but worth noting.
findings.suggestion: Consider using a typed error class (e.g., UnsupportedDocumentTypeError) with the path stored as a property rather than embedded in the message string, allowing callers to decide what to expose.

positive_observations: text-chunker is a clean pure function (Functional Core) with thorough input validation and clear boundary conditions
FolderMonitor properly handles errors in onChange callbacks by wrapping in try/catch and forwarding to onError
FolderMonitor.start() is idempotent and stop() gracefully handles individual unsubscribe failures
Types are well-documented with JSDoc and use readonly properties for immutable result objects
Integration test (document-pipeline) uses real LanceDB with temp directories -- correct pattern per project rules
Test Double for embedding in integration test includes proper rationale comment
DocumentProcessor uses dynamic import for heavy dependencies (pdfjs-dist, mammoth, exceljs), reducing startup cost
Text chunker test suite covers edge cases: empty input, exact chunkSize, overlap verification, and invalid parameter validation
